title: Buchnera rupestris
intro: Buchnera rupestris, also known as Rock Centaury, is a perennial plant species in the Gentianaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Buchnera rupestris include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Buchnera rupestris is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Buchnera rupestris is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a type of hemiparasitic plant, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing other plants. The classification of Buchnera rupestris is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Lamiales, family - Orobanchaceae, genus - Buchnera, species - B. rupestris. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification is based on its botanical characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.
